entry.title: Spend Analysis Week
entry.content:
Week kicked off with a finance-heavy push on epiphany and a wave of App Store prep across echo and nyc.

monday

Epiphany got a new Spend Analysis dashboard and split its coarse discretionary categories into finer ones like Starbucks and liquor. Spark’s recovery work wrapped with an MIT license added. Echo fixed TestFlight upload bugs around bundle type and category, and got renamed to Echo Transcribe.

tuesday

Epiphany shipped to the App Store. Fixed an iOS portfolio double-counting bug, patched up auth tests, and prepped macOS App Store entitlements and a beta lane along the way; SnapTrade sync phantom holdings stay a known post-launch fix. Dose picked up the portfolio vibe palette, fixed a nav header overlap, wired an interaction checker into its logging flow, and added an accent-colored pulse animation. Brief migrated case data to Supabase and finalized multi-user support, plus fixed a missing DNS record. Nimble added a Wikidata layer for direct factual answers. Books got a chapter summaries pipeline and a Project Map SVG fix. Lingo launched a SwiftUI iOS companion app. The portfolio site picked up a responsive featured projects grid and a Talli branding update. Nyc got renamed to NYC Survive with build fixes ahead of TestFlight. Canlii-app got scaffolded as a new project.

wednesday

Spark added iOS, macOS, and watchOS companion apps and bumped to v2.1.0. Brief fixed the leverage widget and made the timeline date labels dynamic. Books synced the Agentic AI chapter 12 summary.

thursday

Lingo got renamed LingoAce and picked up a full UI vibe refresh, icon chip layout, spring animations, and a login fix. Mac listing got metadata to unblock submission. Added screenshots to the README.

friday

Spark expanded its roadmap with remaining App Store tasks and noted submission status in the README. Brief added a roadmap section.

saturday

FloatingTabBar, SF symbols, symbolEffect, and haptics landed across epiphany, spark, dose, brief, and talli in a coordinated sweep. Epiphany hit v2.5.1 removing the map greyscale overlay on web, iOS, and macOS, plus a floating tab fix and a .ship.json for the /ship skill. Spark got v2.1.1 with the same floating tab polish and haptics on vote buttons, plus Sign in with Apple and OAuth stubs in the auth sheet. Lingo shipped v1.1.0: accounts, masterclass unlock, streak sync to iOS, modal login overlay, and a build fix for AnyJSON types. Dose added Sign in with Apple. Brief fixed a live projection rendering bug where checklist and lawyer DB loads weren’t awaited, and added Sign in with Apple and FloatingTabBar in v1.0.1. Echo bumped the macOS build and fixed the app icon border. Talli hit v2.4.3 with FloatingTabBar and macOS App Store signing prep. Books added ML for Dummies ch4-6 and AI Investing for Dummies summaries. The portfolio got a full redesign: nathanu.com sidebar aesthetic, monochrome, project table, and Lingo swapped in for School.

sunday

Auth week. ToS and GitHub OAuth landed across epiphany, dose, and spark in one sweep – all pending Supabase provider setup to go live. Epiphany added map location search on iOS and macOS, fixed Sign in with Apple to use the real endpoint, and bumped to v2.6.0. Brief shipped v6.4.0 with a data-ready signal so PDF and screenshot exports wait for real data before rendering; token PDFs now include checklist and lawyer state for accurate projections. Lingo got auth wired to Supabase on iOS and macOS, streak sync, and a modal login overlay. Books picked up Newton’s biography and two more Dummies series summaries. The portfolio swapped notes/ for a redirect to notes.heyitsmejosh.com, added real app icons, and trimmed the about copy to focus on UVic. Canlii updated its web UI.

monday

Icon cleanup day. Alpha channels stripped from macOS and watchOS icons across epiphany, echo, spark, lingo, and nyc ahead of ASC submission. Epiphany patched the SnapTrade phantom holdings bug and portfolio math. Talli replaced monospace with DM Sans and Fraunces across the web app.

apps

epiphany shipped to App Store + Spend Analysis + v2.6.0 map search + GitHub OAuth + SnapTrade fix + icon fixes, echo TestFlight fixes + renamed Echo Transcribe + icon fixes, dose vibe palette + FloatingTabBar + Sign in with Apple + GitHub OAuth, brief Supabase migration + multi-user + FloatingTabBar + v6.4.0 data-ready export + Sign in with Apple, spark MIT license + iOS/macOS/watchOS companions + v2.2.0 GitHub OAuth + FloatingTabBar + icon fixes, nimble Wikidata layer, books chapter summaries + ML for Dummies + AI Investing + Newton, lingo renamed LingoAce + UI refresh + v1.1.0 Supabase auth + streak sync + modal login + icon fixes, nyc renamed NYC Survive + icon fixes, talli v2.4.3 FloatingTabBar + macOS App Store prep + DM Sans font, nulljosh.github.io sidebar redesign + real icons + notes redirect + about refresh, canlii-app web UI update.

entry.title: Splitting Back Apart
entry.content:
The apps monorepo came apart this week. Everything went back to standalone repos, with the small experiments consolidated into one labs repo instead of scattered on their own.

monday

Tally synced its i18n locales, adding RDSP and CDB translation keys. Echo got a redesigned app icon.

tuesday

Brief came back fully restored across iOS, macOS, and web. Dose got cleaned up as a standalone project, and nimble, nyc, grapher, and wiretext all merged into a single labs repo instead of living as separate small repos.

wednesday

Tally cleaned up the school dashboard, fixed a Capilano typo, and marked Pre-Calc 12 done. Echo’s docs got simplified ahead of its App Store push, and dose dropped npm test from its GitHub Pages workflow.

thursday

The big day. The portfolio site got a full homepage redesign with featured project cards, a live strip, and a timeline, plus a palette swap to blue/black/white/gray. Repo links there now point at the consolidated labs repo. Dotfiles fixed Project Map SVGs that weren’t rendering. Brief replaced a pin bypass with an opaque scrape token and fixed stale docs. Tally generalized its PWD/RDSP/CDB endpoints into one data-driven schema instead of three near-duplicates. Echo’s web app came back online at echo.heyitsmejosh.com. Journal got re-separated into its own repo again.

friday

Epiphany’s iOS Markets view got a polish pass: sparkline ticker on the full drawer, an ellipsis filter menu, flush list rows, company names instead of just tickers. Autopilot’s live mode got capped to a BTC probe instead of running against the full stock watchlist.

saturday

Echo’s Release build got fixed, an invalid suppressTokens assignment was breaking it. Epiphany lost two dead files (simBenchmark.js, stocks-debug.js) and fixed gaps in Langley crime data, flight details, and event/place metadata. Tally added avatar persistence, dropped a misleading $/hr accrual figure, and shipped its App Store privacy manifest with automated screenshots.

sunday

Spark’s full source came back from a Vercel deployment-files API recovery, web, api, iOS, macOS, watchOS, widgets, daemon, migrations, and tests all restored. Lingo’s frontend got recovered the same way from its live deployment. Dose confirmed its account-recovery email path against the real Supabase auth.users table. Brief added first-brief authoring and finalized multi-user support. Talli fixed macOS sign-in to surface the real BC Self-Serve login error instead of a generic session-expired message. Echo made its settings icon more visible and tuned down the transcript scroll feel. Epiphany fixed a flights fallback timeout, an NYC IP-geo bug, and disabled a broken Trade tab. The portfolio site got a copy rewrite with expanded project bullets. Dotfiles moved the Cloudflare token out of tracked config into an untracked secrets file.

Follow-up work today: Brief migrated case data to Supabase across iOS and macOS. Dose wired an interaction checker into logging, fixed nav header overlap on desktop, and applied the portfolio vibe palette. Echo moved the live-recording hero screenshot. Epiphany added ASC macOS cleanup tasks and fixed the map permanently stranding on NYC, plus seeded a feature tracker. Lingo got full README and CLAUDE.md after recovery. Nimble added a Wikidata direct-answer layer. NYC fixed a stale target name and set the app display name. Spark grouped the pill nav into a unified card and marked seed-ideas done. Portfolio site trimmed copy and promoted NYC. Books removed the Pre-Calc summary. Dotfiles updated its changelog to v2.0.0.

June is hot this year, 30C most days, average for the month but still sweltering when it sticks around. Development across the apps is going well and the workflow itself keeps improving, CI, code review, the whole loop is getting smoother and it has been genuinely fun. Got accepted into Capilano University for legal studies. The plan is law first, 3-4 years practicing while keeping math and code going in the background, then a UVic comp sci BSc somewhere around 30, mirroring finance the whole way through, law then comp-sci then finance. Three projects are shipped now and the focus shifts to polishing and monetizing them instead of just building. Still chipping at code box gaps and a few lost steps. Father’s Day tomorrow. Next up is more code review, more tests, math studying, and styling passes.

apps

tally i18n sync + PWD/RDSP/CDB schema unification + App Store privacy manifest, echo icon refresh + web app restore + Release build fix + hero screenshot reordered, dose cleanup + interaction logging + portfolio vibe palette, brief restored + Supabase migration (iOS/macOS) + scrape token security + multi-user authoring, labs consolidation, epiphany Markets polish + Autopilot BTC cap + map fix + feature tracker + ASC cleanup, spark full source recovery + pill nav consolidation, lingo frontend recovery + docs, talli macOS sign-in fix, nimble Wikidata layer, nyc target name fix + app naming, books cleanup, dotfiles v2.0.0, journal re-separated.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio: full homepage redesign, blue/black/white/gray palette, NYC promoted, copy trimmed, links updated.

entry.title: Fixes and Shipping
entry.content:
Focused on shipping and fixes. Epiphany hit the App Store, five follow-up bug fixes rolled out, and the rest of the portfolio caught up on neglected corners.

sunday

Epiphany went live on the App Store after months of revision cycles. The build that shipped removed the Visa debt warning, fixed the portfolio calculation, and locked down the watchOS widget to work offline. TestFlight beta opened the same day for public testing.

monday

Tally hit 3.5.0 with the PWD deadline bump and personal account reference categories for RDSP and CDB. The app no longer guesses at benefit types — it reads directly from BC Self-Serve.

tuesday

Fixed the iOS refresh animation. Network errors on pull-down now display properly instead of disappearing, and retries handle cancelled and timeout cases correctly.

wednesday

Spark’s manual deployment path is ready. Supabase migrations are queued, the service-role key is rotated, and the daemon install is next. Two blockers remain before the prod push.

thursday

Echo got the DNS plumbing finished. Cloudflare DNS updated with the CNAME to nulljosh.github.io, and the relay is ready to unblock OpenSky for Epiphany flights.

friday

SnapTrade sync validation deployed. The fix from earlier in the week went live in production, and OpenSky relay is the next verification step.

apps

apps: epiphany on App Store (Visa debt gone, portfolio fixed, watchOS offline), tally 3.5.0 (PWD deadline + account refs), iOS refresh animation (network errors + retries), spark deployment path (migrations + rotated key-role), echo DNS + OpenSky relay, snaptrade sync fix live.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio: Epiphany now listed as App Store product.

entry.title: Groundwork
entry.content:
Quieter than the week before. The changes went deeper instead of wider: real databases replacing placeholders, auth flows finished end to end, and courses with actual structure.

sunday

bcgd flipped from a static site to a real operations platform, Supabase now backing the inventory and order data. That brings it from a landing page to something usable. Grapher picked up visible zoom controls across web, iOS, and macOS. Charters got the App Store mobile scroll fix. Beep hit bot detection on the transit site and reverted to the iframe approach for now.

monday

Tally got smarter about monthly reporting. It now reads whether you already submitted a report from BC Self-Serve instead of guessing, and the manual toggle was tightened so it stops re-adding what was already removed. Brief got an amendment to the first case, adding the ICBC claim, mother’s assistance during the incident, and the high-function PTSD dimension.

wednesday

Dose got a password reset flow on web. Both sides: the forgot-password request and the recovery page that handles the link from the email. It was missing before.

thursday

The iOS side of Dose caught up. Sign-up, forgot password, and password reset through the deep link all connect now. Auth wired into the sync flow and the settings screen. Tally removed a fake debt triage section and replaced the reporting banner with an Already Filed button.

weekend

Lingo shipped its first real academic course. A full calculus sequence replaced the flat question bank, structured with a proper skill tree. School linked the Pre-Calc cram sheets for modules two through four directly from the dashboard.

Reconnected with Jayda for the first time in about ten years. Three days together, a couple of drives out to Vancouver to catch the sunset. Fun, but also exhausting in a way that’s hard to explain. The whole stretch was one long memory lane, and what it mostly reminded me of is how much I value my own space, energy, and peace now.

The bigger move was submitting the application to Capilano University for Paralegal Studies. That’s the next concrete step toward practicing law.

apps

apps: bcgd Supabase backend (v4.0.0), dose password reset plus iOS auth, lingo calculus course (v1.4.0), school cram sheet links, tally report detection and UI cleanup, brief CASE-0001 ICBC amendment, charters scroll, grapher zoom controls.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio: lingo added to projects list.

entry.title: Fold
entry.content:
Structural week. I folded most of my projects into one monorepo, moved the journal onto Vercel, and rolled a shared design system across every web app. Brief picked up a third case, and the weekend turned into a long Epiphany sprint with Life and Echo coming up behind it.

monday

Brief A+ pass. Priority cards, a sorted checklist, Harding and Chantler promoted to the top. Marked Arvay Finlay declined after Robin Gage passed on the 25th. Tally moved the PWD deadline to the June 18 extension date. Added TLS inspection detection so the school app shows a real error on locked-down WiFi instead of failing silently. Expanded the portfolio reading list to 80 ranked books.

tuesday

Brief got a full v6 redesign, a clean single-case layout. Raised the RCMP damages ceiling to four or five million with new comparables. Activated the third case with a municipal liability and section 285 countdown, officer name held pending ATIP. A slim print mode took the brief PDF from thirty pages down to seven. Pulled in the Surrey Memorial records and updated the evidence checklist.

wednesday

Shipped the CFA palette across every web app, cream, red, yellow, charcoal. NYC got sound effects on macOS, haptics on iOS, and difficulty levels on web. Wiretext shipped PNG export and component search on all three platforms. Added Supabase auth and moved my profile onto a real account. Security pass too: pulled a tracked Vercel token, hardened the session cookie, fixed some CORS. App icons landed on every iOS app.

thursday

The big one. Folded school, cadence, epiphany, and bcgd into the apps monorepo, then moved the journal onto Vercel from inside it. Brief shipped v6.2.0 with an auto-deploy hook and resilient init. Renamed the macOS journal app and fixed a half-rendered icon.

friday

The longest day of the week. Tally got a real internationalization pass, a translation pipeline plus a locale runtime, shipped as v3.4.0. Epiphany got the most attention: I moved the stock data off the retired endpoint onto the FMP stable API everywhere, web plus the iOS and macOS clients, brought the blob cache back, got the test suite green again, and refreshed the whitepaper. Started wiring a SnapTrade broker sync so the holdings can come from a real account instead of being typed in.

Brief kept moving. The third case got its web port, I reconciled the first case across web, iOS, and macOS, propagated the section 19 limitation, and pulled the damages ceiling back to a defensible two to three million from the looser number earlier in the week. Brief now auto-deploys on every change.

School got a polish pass. True white light mode across every page, a device-auto theme that follows the system, a low-ink print mode, and JSON schema validation on the grades and quizzes data so a bad edit fails loudly instead of silently. NYC now starts paused so the player drives from the first frame. The portfolio picked up a library checkout tracker with a due-date countdown. Captured the PDF task lists into the project roadmaps and redacted some PII out of a tally fixture along the way.

saturday

A full day on Epiphany. The map took a Gotham pass: locked the grayscale, dropped the toggle, and raised the marker caps about threefold, then I split places from events so the cards read cleaner and killed the flicker that was making it stutter. News is cached now, the macro view pulls a full set of twelve FRED series through a keyless path, and there is a real PDF spending importer. Live flight tracking went in through OpenSky on proper OAuth2 auth, with the timeout pushed out so the planes actually load on a cold start. Stock detail on iOS and macOS shows a Buy, Hold, or Sell call at a glance, fundamentals load again through a crumb-authenticated path so price to earnings and market cap populate, and the native apps follow system light and dark on their own. Pulled the AI features back out since there is no key in play and pointed the roadmap at the free API setup instead.

Life came together, the therapy document for Amanda. Applied the latest edits across web and iOS, culled four redundant charts so the views earn their space, and did a parity and privacy cleanup so both platforms read the same.

Echo reached the submission line. The App Store prep and packages are assembled and a privacy policy page is up for review. Tally rode along with its own submission package and an i18n test suite on top of the translation pipeline. Lingo picked up a skill-tree path and a Computers track.

apps

apps: monorepo fold, journal Vercel cutover, brief v6.2.0 plus CASE-0003 and a $2-3M ceiling, CFA palette, Supabase auth, tally i18n through v3.4.1 with a submission package, epiphany shipping hard from the FMP migration through the Gotham map, OpenSky flights, cached news, twelve FRED series, Buy/Hold/Sell calls and SnapTrade sync, life on web and iOS for Amanda, echo at the App Store submission line, school theme and schema work, NYC, lingo, and wiretext feature drops, iOS icons across the board.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio: CFA design system sitewide, true-white light mode, a library checkout tracker, brief case corrections, reading list expanded to 80 titles, Currently section updated.

entry.title: Glass
entry.content:
Charters got liquid glass and shipped iOS. Brief’s authentication got rebuilt and CASE-0002 got its full treatment. Two new apps landed Saturday – Echo and Beep. Snow leopard pass ran across every project in the codebase. Busy week.

monday

Brief’s authentication got rebuilt. Magic link was fragile, replaced it with a standard password flow and two-step login. Paul Kent and DLA Law both passed, marked as declined, two new referrals added – Harding and Chantler. The lawyer contact section also picked up biometric cooldown logic.

tuesday

Life got new content. Conscience, MDMA, LSD, and Concerta sections added across web and iOS. The iOS side had broken section-to-chart mappings that I traced down and fixed – small thing but it was causing silent data gaps.

wednesday

Charters hit v1.1.0. The web version got the liquid glass treatment – Geist font, monochrome palette – then the iOS app came together the same day with fifteen countries and full data parity. Compare mode landed alongside it.

thursday

Applied to Capilano University for the BLS Paralegal diploma. Got the details documented in school notes and dotfiles. Brief picked up lawyer contact details and biometric cooldown logic.

friday

Brief CASE-0002 continued – synced scenarios and comparables with updated projections, removed the Supabase journal, fixed the macOS toolbar label. Then an A+ cleanup pass: CASE-0002 bleed fixes, white theme, dead code removed. Tally got a blob migration fix – legacy path fallback restored and the payment amount regex broadened. Snow leopard pass started across the codebase.

saturday

Two new apps. Echo is a native on-device Whisper transcription app for iOS and macOS – file import, drag and drop, waveform feedback, language selection, light/dark adaptive, no server required. Beep is a fully native SwiftUI Compass card app – Face ID biometric login, real SPA backend with balance and trips, two-step auth on both iOS and web. Renamed from compass-ios. Snow leopard pass finished across the remaining twenty-plus projects in the monorepo. Vitest suites added for wiretext and grapher.

sunday

Beep picked up a final WKWebView bridge fix – switched to continuation-based JS evaluation, which clears the blank dashboard that showed up on Face ID return. Portfolio’s GitHub stats panel broke when the CORS proxies went stale; replaced with server-side fetching via GitHub Actions. Cameron Ward officially confirmed not practising – marked declined in Brief.

apps

apps (echo, beep, charters, brief, life, tally): two new apps shipped (echo, beep), codebase-wide quality pass, brief CASE-0002 complete.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio: Brief updates tracking lawyer statuses, CASE-0002 phase two, CapU enrollment documented, GitHub stats fetching moved server-side.

entry.title: Filed
entry.content:
The brief went native. A full Swift app in a week – dark launch screen, tabs, damage tables, Wang v. AG Canada in the comparables. The web version got the same treatment: global comparables, father’s witness statement, limitation defense, Ward framework, per-head stack. It’s a real tool now.

sunday

Tally hit v3.5.0. Message read state now syncs cross-platform – iOS reads what web marked, web reads what iOS marked. The MessagesView got a full redesign. Avatar disk cache added so it stops refetching on every render. Three commits to land stable message IDs before the read state could work at all.

monday

Dose got a vibe pass. Plus Jakarta Sans headings, warm dark palette – #161514 base, #edebe8 text. Auto dark/light mode and an iOS build fix. Nimble picked up natural language query improvements and version display in macOS Settings.

tuesday

Life integrated 2019-2024 personal history across web and iOS. That data was always in my head; now it’s in the app. Langley Hospital corrected (not Lions Gate – different hospital, different incident). Fuse removed from apps, claude-usage dropped, roost and spark vision expanded in the README.

wednesday – thursday

Brief iOS v1.0.0. Built from scratch in Swift: three tabs (overview, grounds, money), comparable cases table, risk color coding, a scales icon, dark launch screen. Fixed Swift 6 @Observable issues, ShapeStyle extension, UIRequiresFullScreen. Wang v. AG Canada landed in the native comparables table the same day it went into the web version.

Web brief kept moving in parallel: global comparable awards added, father’s witness statement with legal annotations, risks and evidence gaps section, lawyers section, wrist injury documented, draft email template, limitation defense analysis integrated, Ward framework for general damages, per-head stack for household claims. Roadmap for v3 multi-user auth drafted.

friday

Applied to Capilano University for the paralegal diploma. Suiting up.

apps

apps (brief-ios, tally, dose, nimble, life): 20+ commits across the week. Brief was the anchor – every other app got a pass while it was building.

nulljosh.github.io

Portfolio brief page expanded heavily. It started as a layout experiment two weeks ago; it’s now the most complete documentation of the case that exists anywhere.

entry.title: Hardened
entry.content:
The brief page is a different thing now. Two cases, per-violation breakdowns, sidebar on desktop, the right fonts. The rest of the week: tally marks on the iOS splash, school tracker rebuilt in the tally style, and the Trakt key’s unproductive round trip through a server proxy.

tuesday

Tally iOS redesign. Three commits. Full UI overhaul – orange replaces system blue everywhere, solid cards replace the blur material, notification read-state now syncs from the server on mount. Landing page rebuilt as a hero splash. Then a simplification pass. Finished with the avatar – went from node-graph to pixel-art and back to node-graph before landing on the Anthropic dot-proximity style. iOS home now matches the web: payment progress bar, monthly/next-payment stats grid.

Journal got GitHub Actions CI. ship.sh simplified down to the bits that matter.

wednesday

Brief page on the portfolio got a full redesign. Two cases merged in – Two Daryls and a charter rights settlement analysis. Rebuilt the layout around them: tabs per case, accordion for the legal grounds, per-violation damages breakdown. Inter Tight headers, JetBrains Mono for citations, paper texture. It reads like a legal tool now instead of a code project page.

thursday

Brief’s desktop layout finally has the right shape. The page had been single-column since it launched – fine on mobile but flat on a wide monitor. Sticky hero on the left, case panel beside it.

iOS: dark login screen now, tally marks on the splash. They work better as a launch image than a plain logo – they’re part of what the app does, not just a symbol standing in for it. Pre-Calc 12 switched from online self-paced to a spring structured class finishing mid-June, so the school tracker got updated.

apps

Grapher landed – Desmos-style graphing calculator, no external dependencies. PIN auth moved per-user, stored in an encrypted Blob profile instead of shared state. Messages sync was out of step with report status and account info – fixed all three together.

nulljosh.github.io

The Trakt API key was hardcoded in client JavaScript. Pulled it out and wrote a serverless proxy at /api/trakt. Then Cloudflare blocked the server IP and the stat went blank. Restored it client-side. Net: the key is slightly more exposed but the stat actually works. Will revisit.

Projects trimmed to four shipped – fuse, bcgd, nimble, nyc, wiretext removed from the list. Tighter.

saturday

School dashboard caught up to the rest – orange accent, solid cards, the same weight as tally. It was the only app in the suite still on system blue. A&P 12 done and hidden. Pre-Calc blended reads correctly.

The Trakt proxy that went in early in the week was out by Saturday. Three commits to get back to where we started. Net zero on that one, minus the hours. The brief redesign was a proper afternoon. The iOS splash was fast – tally marks make more sense as a launch image than a plain logo.

entry.title: Fresh Coat
entry.content:
Steady week. Two real builds – a tally design overhaul and Fuse calendar feeds. Everything else was cleanup and fixes.

apps/tally

Full design replacement on Thursday. The phone-frame shell went first – it was constraining the layout and hardcoding amounts that should have been live. New stack: Space Grotesk type, parchment palette with clrs.cc orange accent, a desktop layout that actually uses the viewport. Body background now matches the theme, Screen shell capped at 430px max-width on desktop, centering flex that was squishing content removed.

Data fixes in the same pass. Pay date was stale. Messages section was generating duplicates – deduped. PWD step 3 (medical assessment) marked done. Badge clears on view.

apps/fuse

v1.1.0 landed Friday. The main addition: Google Calendar integration. Paste a secret ICS URL in the new Settings tab and Fuse fetches it through a Vercel proxy to get around CORS. A lightweight ICS parser handles UTC, floating, and all-day dates. Refetch fires via a custom window event when the feed changes. Font swapped from -apple-system to Geist, self-hosted woff2. Bottom nav picked up a Settings tab. Calendar feeds make the app feel like it was built for something. Before that it was countdowns into a void.

nulljosh.github.io

Vibe clone from ashwingopalsamy.in on Thursday. Warmer palette: #161514 dark, #ebebeb light. Plus Jakarta Sans for headings. Blue accent (#2558d9 dark / #5b8dee light). Heatmap cells scale to the blue. Box-shadows removed, links de-italicized, section labels use the accent color.

cadence

Per-repo breakdown in the stats view – individual commit counts per repo instead of aggregate totals. Swift fix on the API layer.

vercel

Hit the 12-function limit in the apps monorepo. comment-counts.js was a one-query endpoint in its own file. Merged it into comments.js. Under the limit.

apps/life

Therapy session notes logged Monday. Religion section extended with the theodicy argument. Autism section added the lifespan gap data (16 years shorter on average, 9x suicide rate) and the gut-brain axis mechanics. GI section updated. Housing history expanded – the door removal at 16. All mirrored to iOS.

friday

May 1. Most of this week was polish. Nothing brand new except Fuse calendar feeds, and that one moved fast – scaffold to shipped in a day. Tally looks completely different from last week. The portfolio keeps cycling through design iterations but the content is more accurate than it’s been.

Pre-calc test prep is overdue. Getting to it this weekend.

entry.title: Loose Ends
entry.content:
Slow through Wednesday, then Thursday opened up. Wednesday I kept opening things and closing them. Nothing stuck – not a bad mood, just flat. Then Thursday I stopped waiting for it and started working anyway, and it came. Most of the real work landed in the last two days.

apps/tally

Tally moved into the apps monorepo a week ago. A few things broke in transit. Footer links still pointed at the old standalone GitHub repo – fixed to the monorepo path. iOS target had two variables declared var that were never mutated; flipped them to let, regenerated the xcodeproj so Xcode would finally see SettingsView. Added ROADMAP.md with a live brain dump: earning-per-hour drift, a scaling earnings graph, and a single config field for income bumps.

v3.1.0 landed Thursday. Three additions: a DTC application tracker (filed April 2026, sitting at the CRA processing step), a CRA upcoming payments section with GST/HST $174.50 flagged for June 5, and a timing warning that blocks report submission before the 28th of the period. PWD bumped to week 8, decision pending. All four went into the web app and the iOS SwiftUI target at the same time.

Design system pass followed. Amounts count up on first render, a bar tracks the 30-day cycle, the dot shifts amber near payday, paid amounts fade back. Tab switches fade. None of it was strictly necessary but the page stopped feeling static.

nulljosh.github.io

The portfolio got a full rebuild Thursday. The carousel-plus-device-frame layout had become hard to maintain and broke in light mode.

Replaced it with a typographic list: weight-200 hero, prefers-color-scheme auto light/dark with a manual toggle, GitHub contribution heatmap with click-to-expand year popovers, and a live stats strip (films, TV, code, writing, weather, coffee). Added Currently and Background sections. The index.html went from 2,081 lines to a net 655 added (1,426 removed). Cadence added to the project list while in there. Two follow-up fixes: flex-wrap on the items list to prevent overflow on mobile, and the Currently section brought in line with the items style.

apps/roost

FavoritesContext was running Array.includes() inside a filter memo – O(n) per listing on every render. Derived a favoriteSet via useMemo, swapped all lookups to Set.has(). Added vercel.json with security headers.

The vibe pass followed: ported the portfolio dark editorial design over. Same treatment as spark – dark background, typographic hierarchy, flat colors.

apps/spark

Got the same vibe pass as roost: portfolio dark editorial design applied end-to-end.

apps/dose

Extracted shortDate, shortTime, and longDate into shared date utility functions. They were inline and duplicated across several views.

Security

Pass across dose, fuse, life, spark, and tally: added X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, X-XSS-Protection, and HSTS to each vercel.json. Hardened .gitignore across the monorepo, dotfiles, and the portfolio repo to block .env variants from staging.

apps/life

The money line was behind – updated to call out trillion-dollar AI labs by name. Version bumped to 4.1.1.

dotfiles

General todos brain-dump committed. Ticked off two completed items: savings prompt and a stale downloads deletion script.

thursday

Pre-calc in the afternoon. Math test coming up, keeping it steady. The week started as loose-end cleanup and turned into a full build session by Thursday night.

friday

Tally refactor: extracted renderTracker() and TimelineCard out of ContentView.swift. Duplicate scaffolding was spread across three tracker types. Collapsed to two functions.

Submitted the remaining school assignments. Science project still needs to go in – that’s next week’s problem.

Did a broader codebase cleanup pass. The Vercel hack earlier this week was a good forcing function. Hardened security headers, tightened .gitignore across everything, audited exposed env vars. Good to have it done.

On the AI tooling side: Opus 4.7 is a disappointment. GPT 5.5 is decent but not a clear winner. Considering moving more work to Codex. Nothing decided yet.

monday

claude-usage got the biggest update yet. Budget progress bars landed – each limit (session, weekly, extra) now shows $X/$Y with a bar that tracks spend. Then the entire dashboard was rebuilt as a 4-tab React UI from a design handoff. Tabs for Overview, Sessions, History, and Settings. The old single-page layout is gone.

Roost got a pixel art avatar generator – deterministic 8x8 sprites from username hash, renders as SVG. Added a migration step to README for the avatar_url column.

dose: Concerta added to the substances list.

systems restructure: cleared all 21 legacy subsystem implementations (NullOS, nullC, voxel-engine, blockchain, bittorrent, container-runtime, physics-engine, and 14 others) from the repo. The implementations were years of from-scratch proofs-of-concept. Repo now holds only top-level config and docs. Crash last night corrupted the git index mid-commit – fixed with a manual object rehash on startup this morning, then pushed clean.

sunday

Cleared out a backlog of project notes that had piled up in Downloads as PDFs – one per repo, each just “next steps?” and a sentence or two. Triaged the lot, deleted nulljosh/path (stub repo, never used), updated roadmaps across nyc-web, bcgd, spark, dose, and tally with the outstanding items.

Also found the cadence tracking question: the journal git history was squashed to a single commit earlier in the month, which nuked cadence’s commit graph for that repo. Nothing to fix – cadence tracks forward from here.

School tracking updated. All Pre-Calc 12 learning guides done (units 1–7). Biology 12 all done – nine units, every project submitted. The school web dashboard now shows the full unit breakdown and the UVic transcript submission (Apr 23). Only thing left is the pre-calc module tests. Need 77%+ for UVic CS admission.

Also redeployed bcgd/web – it had been redirecting to the Vercel login screen since Apr 14. Fixed with a fresh prod deploy. Laid out a prioritized multi-session roadmap covering NYC difficulty levels, epiphany critical fixes, cadence API wiring, tally payment chart, and a bcgd site clone.

Portfolio got a second pass. Wiretext and Fuse added to the portfolio project list, projects reordered by shipped quality, Currently section updated to match where things actually are.

NYC city sim got a difficulty selector: Easy, Medium, Hard. Easy starts with generous resources, Hard starts constrained. Makes the game actually worth replaying from a blank slate.

Tally CRA payments section got sparkline bars – a small horizontal bar behind each amount scaled relative to the largest payment. Pure visual, no data change.

Cadence submodule ref bumped to pull an iOS/macOS API fix.

Deleted browser, browser-ios, and nyc-wallpaper from the apps monorepo. All three were placeholder scaffolds with no active development. Dead weight, gone.
